entries.name: The Paintress & The Monolith
entries.content: The Paintress is a mysterious, god-like entity residing at the Monolith. Once a year, she awakens to paint a new number on the stone. This number dictates the Gommage. She appears as a colossal, wooden mannequin-like figure with long white hair. While she is viewed as a bringer of death, she is actually a grieving mother (Aline Dessendre) who created the 'Canvas' (this world) to protect the souls of those lost in the real world.

entries.name: The Gommage (Erasure)
entries.content: The Gommage is the annual event where the Paintress erases everyone whose age matches the number she painted on the Monolith. Those erased vanish instantly, turning into wisps of smoke or flower petals. The Gommage is a countdown toward human extinction. Currently, the number is descending toward the youngest ages, forcing the people of Lumière into a desperate struggle for survival.
entries.comment: The central threat of the world

entries.name: Lumière & The Fracture
entries.content: Lumière is the last bastion of humanity, a city inspired by Belle Époque Paris. It was isolated from the rest of the continent by 'The Fracture,' a cataclysmic event that shattered reality. The city is governed by a Council that organizes the 'Expeditions'—suicide missions to reach the Monolith and destroy the Paintress. Lumière is a place of refined culture and deep, underlying dread.

entries.name: The Canvas (The Painted World)
entries.content: The world the Expeditioners inhabit is not 'real'—it is a 'Canvas,' a painted reality created by Aline Dessendre. This world is fueled by Chroma (magical paint essence) and is populated by Nevrons and Axons. The Canvas was created to cope with grief, but it is being corrupted from the outside by the Curator (Renoir), who seeks to break the illusion and force Aline to face the real world.

entries.name: The 33rd Expedition
entries.content: Expedition 33 is the latest group of warriors and scholars tasked with ending the Paintress's cycle. Led by Gustave, the group includes Maelle, Lune, and others. Each expeditioner joins for their own reasons—some for honor, some for revenge, and some, like Maelle, to find an identity before they are erased. They follow the 'Pathfinder' (Lune) through the surreal landscapes of the Canvas toward the Citadel.
